description:
Analyze household size distributions and housing types across San Francisco to identify 'family-rich' zones using the Jupyter notebook at /home/ga/urbansim_projects/notebooks/family_housing_assessment.ipynb. Using the 'households', 'buildings', and 'parcels' tables from /home/ga/urbansim_projects/data/sanfran_public.h5, identify the top 15 zones with the highest concentration of family households (defined as 3 or more persons). Link households to zones via buildings and parcels, filling missing residential unit counts with 0. Classify housing as Single-Family (1 unit) or Multi-Family (>1 unit) and filter for zones with at least 50 total households.

Export the top 15 zones by family concentration rate to /home/ga/urbansim_projects/output/top_family_zones.csv including columns for zone_id, total_households, total_family_households, family_in_sf, family_in_mf, and family_concentration_rate. Save citywide summary metrics to /home/ga/urbansim_projects/output/family_housing_summary.json with keys: 'citywide_total_households', 'citywide_family_households', 'citywide_family_rate', and 'most_family_dense_zone_id'. Generate a chart comparing family households in single-family vs multi-family buildings for the top 15 zones at /home/ga/urbansim_projects/output/family_housing_distribution.png. The notebook must be fully executed with all outputs visible.
